author: .paulharris
content:
I noticed `Nimbus EL` is reporting with the same client code we had expected nimbus CL to report with in graffiti watermarking, which is kind of a collision given the order in watermarks wasn't guaranteed...
We probably need to make them unique if we want the EL/CL order on watermarks to be not important, as atm in devnet6 it kind of looks like 2 CL's in the watermark `lighthouse-nimbusel-1 NB94LH0d`

author: tersec
content: sure, well, Nimbus is following https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/blob/v1.0.0-beta.7/src/engine/identification.md#clientcode but that list can be changed

author: .paulharris
content: I've not seen any Nimbus CL blocks including it at all, but i did notice the nimbus-el on testnet

author: tersec
content: because it's in the execution API specs

author: .paulharris
content: so the NB I can always treat as Nimbus EL?

author: .paulharris
content: NB was originally listed as the nimbus CL is all.

author: tersec
content: Well, practically right now. Currently by default Nimbus CL would use it absent any other allocation if we, e.g., merge https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth2/pull/8620 (which I need to look at again, it's probably alright)

author: .paulharris
content: it'd be a collsion if CL and EL are both using it... so maybe the answer is CL uses a different code...

author: tersec
content: that's one option, yes. The EL could change its code, also, either way. I do agree they should be distinct

author: marioevz
content:
### EL breakout summary (AI)

-   **EIP-7928 / PR [#11854](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11854)**: The group discussed the SSTORE access cost check update. There was no opposition, and <@830368514225340436>  is handling the corresponding EELS spec changes.

-   **EIP-2780 / PR [#11844](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11844)**: The main discussion focused on changing account creation charging to happen conditionally at access time rather than unconditionally up front.

  -   **Key concern raised**: This change may alter prior behavior around **EIP-7702 authorizations** — previously, authorizations were still applied even if the top-level frame ran out of gas, and the group wanted to confirm whether that should still hold under the new approach. The plan was to proceed with changes and in parallel check with <@543900561460822016> before fully settling that point.

-   **EIP-8037 / PR [#11858](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11858)**: The room generally supported the change because it avoids overcharging and makes the charging behavior more coherent with the previously discussed change.

-   **Spec mismatch noted by Gary**: <@402853454126776320> flagged a mismatch between the written EIP and spec implementation, which <@755913785620824175>  said team would track and update in the tracker: https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/35279#issuecomment-4873297759
    
-   **EIP-8282 / [sys-asm#49](https://github.com/ethereum/sys-asm/pull/49) **: The call briefly mentioned the PR to add logic to disable a contract through a system call. Spec updates are work in progress.

author: lightclient
content: sorry i wasn't on the call today. so currently the auths are expected to land as long as the tx is valid, regardless of what happens with the execution. this gives the mempool the ability to statically reason about the nonce of non-delegated who are trying to delegate, since if the tx is valid you know what the nonce of the account will be. i don't think it would be an issue here to roll back the delegation / update since we already start treating accounts with in-flight delegations as special in the mempool and have the nonce not update still costs the same, so doesn't seem like a DOS issue. the bigger issue is probably downstream tooling the determines if a delegation landed. i'm not sure exactly how they implement it. if they look at the actual code in the account it is fine, but imagine some validate the auth itself and then check if the tx hash of the including tx is on chain and then consider it included. these are the main potential issues i can think of
